title: Network-wide flood management for a motorway operator
summary: CEMS flood maps gave a motorway operator a network-wide inundation picture during a major flood, improving closure decisions.
challenge: During a major flood, the operator had to make closure decisions across hundreds of km at once. Road-weather sensors were submerged or offline, and incident reports were too slow and localised.
approach: CEMS Rapid Mapping delivered Sentinel-1-derived flood-extent maps within hours of the peak, updated twice daily over several days and integrated into the GIS-based traffic-management system.
results: Many closure and reopening decisions were made using the maps, with post-event review confirming high accuracy against on-site inspection. Several unnecessary closures were avoided, maintaining emergency access.
